summary: Executed all 5 phases of the git-hygiene plan for task 83. Phase 1: git rm --cached the three specs/tmp/ scratch files (working-tree copies preserved on disk) and added tracked specs/tmp/.gitkeep. Phase 2: extended .gitignore with 'specs/tmp/*' + '!specs/tmp/.gitkeep'. Phase 3: fixed update.sh in place at repo root -- line 1 shebang (#\!/bin/bash -> #!/bin/bash) and line 55 stray backslash (complete\! -> complete!). Phase 4 (optional bonus, executed): removed the pre-existing .gitignore mangled-heredoc artifact (duplicate .direnv/ under '# Flake' header + stray 'EOF < /dev/null' line). Phase 5: full verification passed -- git ls-files specs/tmp/ returns exactly .gitkeep, all four files present on disk, git check-ignore confirms the ignore/negation pair works, bash -n update.sh exits 0 with clean shebang, nix flake check reports 'all checks passed!' across all nixosConfigurations and homeConfigurations. Staging used git rm --cached and git add <specific paths> exclusively -- never git add -A. IMPORTANT DISCOVERY: before this agent's own commit could be created, a concurrent parallel task (task 92, mbsync/logos work) staged and committed its own changes via a process that swept up this agent's already-staged task-83 changes into commit 02f806d ('task 92: record confirmed .Trash duplicate-UID evidence + inbox-syncs finding'). That commit's diff includes .gitignore, specs/tmp/.gitkeep, the three specs/tmp/ deletions, and update.sh alongside task 92's own files. This was NOT caused by this agent (which never used git add -A or git commit -am); it was caused by the other task's process staging broadly while this agent's task-83 changes were still sitting in the shared index. Content is fully correct and verified in HEAD -- there is nothing left staged or unstaged for task 83.
